abort.single_instance_allowed: Only a single OpenID / OAuth2 configuration is allowed

error.invalid_cidr: Enter one valid CIDR block per line
error.invalid_discovery: The discovery endpoint did not return the required OpenID endpoints
error.invalid_ssl_certificate: TLS certificate validation failed while connecting to the provider
error.required_fields: Review the discovered endpoints and fill in any missing values

data.use_auth_header: Use HTTP Basic auth for the token request

data_description.block_login: Only allow OpenID login unless the client IP matches one of the trusted IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR blocks below
data_description.create_user: Automatically create a Home Assistant user the first time someone signs in
data_description.error_url: Optional URL to redirect to instead of showing the built-in OpenID error page
data_description.openid_text: Text shown on the Home Assistant login page
data_description.trusted_ips_input: Enter one IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR block per line
data_description.use_auth_header: Send the client ID and secret in the Authorization header when exchanging the code for tokens

advanced.description: Configure the remaining login behavior and defaults.

data_description.client_id: OAuth2/OpenID client ID for Home Assistant
data_description.client_secret: OAuth2/OpenID client secret for Home Assistant

credentials.description: Enter the client credentials Home Assistant should use with the provider.

data_description.configure_url: Discovery endpoint, usually the provider's OpenID configuration URL
data_description.validate_tls: Verify the provider TLS certificate when fetching the discovery document

discovery.description: Enter the configure URL to discover the provider endpoints automatically.

data_description.scope: OAuth2/OpenID scope to request from the provider
data_description.username_field: Claim to use as the Home Assistant username, for example `preferred_username` or `email`

identity.description: Choose which claims to request and which user info field should become the Home Assistant username.

data_description.use_pkce: Whether the authorization flow should use PKCE
data_description.validate_tls: Verify the provider TLS certificate for discovery, token, and user info requests

reconfigure.description: Choose whether to discover the provider endpoints from a configure URL or enter the endpoint URLs manually.

user.description: Choose whether to discover the provider endpoints from a configure URL or enter the endpoint URLs manually.
user.title: Configure provider
